channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Harvard records requested by Education Department
item.description: The records request comes after the Trump administration has made multiple moves against the Ivy League.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump calls FSU shooting a ‘shame,’ says he’ll always protect Second Amendment
item.description: President Trump on Thursday called a deadly shooting at Florida State University (FSU) "terrible," but he dismissed the possibility of it spurring any changes to gun laws in the country. "Look, I'm a big advocate of the Second Amendment. I have been from the beginning.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Confidence in Congressional Democrats hits all time low in new poll
item.description: A new national poll shows that confidence in Democratic Party leaders in Congress has hit an all-time low.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 10,000 pages of records about Robert F. Kennedy's 1968 assassination are released, on Trump's order
item.description: Approximately 10,000 pages of records related to the 1968 assassination of Democratic New York Sen. Robert F.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Minnesota baseball lovers make a pitch to honor bygone ballpark, 2 historic St. Paul teams
item.description: The St. Paul Saints and St. Paul Colored Gophers played ball in the early 1900s at a tiny ballpark near the Capitol called the Pillbox. The Colored Gophers were a vital piece of Black baseball history nationally. A local historian is lobbying now for a public commemoration of the site.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Biden's vax-focused COVID-19 website obliterated by White House, replaced with 'true origins' virus guide
item.description: The White House rolled out a new COVID.gov website showing the “true origins" of the coronavirus after Democrats and the media discredited the lab-leak theory.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump admin to cut 90% of CFPB in latest layoffs: Reports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration is cutting nearly 90 percent of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s (CFPB) employees in a push to significantly downsize the watchdog agency formed to shield U.S. consumers from financial fraud and abuse.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Surprise open governor's seat in Iowa signals competitive GOP primary, glimmer of hope for Democrats
item.description: Iowa looked a lot different in 2010, when Republican Kim Reynolds was first elected to statewide office.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Fingerhut, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Radical activist who fueled Cori Bush's campaigns emerges as spokesperson for track star's murder suspect
item.description: A spokesperson for the family of the 17-year-old boy accused of stabbing a teen track star to death has a long history of supporting defunding the police as well as close ties to a former 'Squad' Democrat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge temporarily restricts DOGE access to personalized Social Security data
item.description: An Obama-appointed judge in Baltimore issued a preliminary injunction Thursday restricting the Department of Government Efficiency’s access to Social Security data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran-US talks over Tehran's nuclear program hinge on a billionaire and a seasoned diplomat
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — As far as biographies go, the two men in charge of the delicate negotiations between Iran and the United States over Tehran's rapidly advancing nuclear program couldn't be more different.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and AMIR VAHDAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Maryland Sen. Van Hollen meets with Abrego Garcia in El Salvador amid court fight over US return
item.description: SAN SALVADOR (AP) — Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen met with Kilmar Abrego Garcia on Thursday evening in El Salvador, coming face to face with the wrongly deported man after two days in the country pushing for his release.
item.dc:creator: YOLANDA MAGAÑA and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Law firms, universities and now civil society groups are in Trump's sights for punitive action
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — First the nation’s top law firms. Then its premier universities. Now, President Donald Trump is leaning on the advocacy groups that underpin U.S. civil society.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge in Baltimore temporarily limits DOGE access to Social Security data
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday imposed new restrictions on billionaire Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, limiting its access to Social Security systems that hold personal data on millions of Americans. U.S.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In their words: What judges and Trump's government say about Abrego Garcia's mistaken deportation
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has dug in on its contention that the government should not have to repatriate Kilmar Abrego Garcia, despite U.S. Supreme Court and lower court rulings that he was wrongly deported and should be returned to the United States.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania senator asked child 'pornography' question during civics competition
item.description: Republican State Sen. David Argall asked the sixth grader if it was "appropriate to give pornographic magazines to kindergarteners.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, Fox 5

item.title: The Abrego Garcia case pulls Democrats into the immigration debate Trump wants to have
item.description: For Democrats, the Kilmar Abrego Garcia case is about fundamental American ideals, including due process, following court orders and preventing government overreach.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves And Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US to withdraw 600 troops from Syria, leaving fewer than 1,000 to help counter IS militants
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. will withdraw about 600 troops from Syria, leaving fewer than 1,000 to work with Kurdish allies to counter the Islamic State group, a U.S. official said Thursday. The official spoke on the condition of anonymity to discuss details not yet announced publicly. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AP and the Trump administration argue over presidential access before appeals judges
item.description: The Associated Press and the Trump administration returned to a courtroom Thursday — and will be in another on Friday — as part of the high-stakes battle over who can control which journalists are able to question the president. Lawyers argued before a three-judge panel of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Letitia James reaps what she sows after leveling 'laughable' case at Trump: expert
item.description: A federal housing agency sent a criminal referral to the Department of Justice Monday arguing New York Attorney General Letitia James appeared to have falsified mortgage records.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Afghanistan Medal of Honor recipient reenlists in Marine Corps reserves after 15 years
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Dakota Meyer, a Marine who was awarded the Medal of Honor for heroism in the Afghanistan War but later became a sharp critic of the Biden administration over its chaotic withdrawal from that conflict, has reenlisted in the military and will serve in the Marine Reserves.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge grants detained U of M student bond but he‘ll stay in ICE custody as feds appeal
item.description: An international student facing deportation was granted bond by a judge earlier this week. However, he will stay locked up after the Department of Homeland Security filed an appeal.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Protesters gather outside El Salvador’s consulate in St. Paul
item.description: Protesters gathered outside El Salvador’s consulate in St. Paul on Wednesday to demand the return of a man who was mistakenly sent to a prison in that Central American nation.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff and The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Barack Obama, Bernie Sanders, Ben Carson among most liked politicians, surveys show
item.description: In one YouGov survey on Democratic figures, 58 percent of respondents said they felt “very favorable” or “somewhat favorable” toward Obama.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Trump policies top the talking points at Minnesota lawmaker town halls
item.description: Lawmakers from both parties fielded questions and comments from constituents in person. President Donald Trump’s policies on immigration, health care and budget cuts generated the most heat.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, Dan Kraker, and Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Former DeSantis spokesperson accused of exposing himself at mall
item.description: A New College of Florida official and former spokesperson for Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis was arrested Tuesday and charged with exposure of sexual organs.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Italian prime minister meets with Trump on tariffs
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni is expected to speak for Italy and the European Union as a whole.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: White House invites Rachel Morin's mother to press briefing to share daughter's story
item.description: The mother of a Maryland woman who was killed by an individual who entered the country illegally addressed reporters at the White House on Wednesday as the Trump administration seeks to go on offense over the case of an individual who was mistakenly deported to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Newsom says California will sue over Trump tariffs
item.description: “President Trump’s unlawful tariffs are wreaking chaos on California families, businesses, and our economy — driving up prices and threatening jobs," Newsom said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Kilmar Abrego Garcia's wife sought restraining order in 2021, DHS says
item.description: The wife of Kilmar Abrego Garcia filed for an order of protection against her husband in 2021, saying he was violent toward her.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: PIX on Politics Daily: NYC mayoral election poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Welcome to PIX on Politics Daily with Dan Mannarino, where we break down the day's political news, headlines, and issues that matter most to you through in-depth conversation. Join us daily on PIX11+ streaming at 1 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Funding for NYC Pre-K and 3-K permanently locked in: Mayor
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- For the first time ever, New York City has permanently locked in funding for its Pre-K and 3-K programming, Mayor Eric Adams announced on Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, PIX 11

item.title: Biden alleges Trump has 'taken a hatchet' to Social Security in his first post-presidency speech
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former President Joe Biden warned on Tuesday that President Donald Trump has “taken a hatchet” to Social Security, weighing in on a critical issue for millions of Americans as the 82-year-old Democrat briefly returned to the national stage.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs executive action to lower drug prices
item.description: The action is intended to build upon the existing program for Medicare drug price negotiations, which was created by the Inflation Reduction Act that passed during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin, MPR News

item.title: Biden says Trump administration has done 'breathtaking' damage to Social Security
item.description: Former President Joe Biden warned a Chicago audience assembled Tuesday at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled that the Trump administration has "taken a hatchet" to Social Security.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Trump plans order to cut funding for NPR and PBS
item.description: President Trump and GOP members of Congress have accused the public broadcasters of biased and "woke" programming. The president plans a rescission, which would give Congress 45 days to approve the directive or allow funding to be restored.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik and Scott Neuman, MPR News

item.title: Bipartisan senators rebuke White House move to end legal aid for unaccompanied minors
item.description: The letter obtained by NPR marks a rare bipartisan critique from Capitol Hill of the administration's immigration policy.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News
